Title: Two cytochromes P450 catalyze S-heterocyclizations in cabbage phytoalexin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Bra009149; GenBank TPA BK009360–BK009375 (CYP71CR family sequences)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Rapid Cycling Brassica rapa (allele chosen by comparison with Chiifu reference genome)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Rapid Cycling Brassica rapa; comparison cultivar Chiifu-401-42
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative S-heterocyclization of brassinin to cyclobrassinin, likely via indole C2,C3 epoxidation followed by sulfur attack at C2 and dehydration
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cyclobrassinin branch of cabbage phytoalexin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream tailoring step from brassinin toward cyclobrassinin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key dedicated pathway step established; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense against pathogens; dietary metabolite with reported antineoplastic activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Elicited leaf microsome assay; RNA-seq candidate identification; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae WAT11 with ATR1; in vitro enzyme assay with NADPH; HPLC-MS/MS product identification; kinetic analysis; deuterium-labeled substrate mechanistic assay; qRT-PCR/metabolite time-course correlation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly upregulated in Brassica rapa leaves after flg22 and Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in elicited RCBr leaves; transcript levels broadly correlate with cyclobrassinin accumulation over time
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ATR1 (heterologous CYP reductase partner in yeast expression)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen elicitation with Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola (Psm); flg22 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful dedicated gene for engineering or breeding enhanced cyclobrassinin production in edible crucifers; candidate for pathway reconstruction with additional upstream/downstream genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Apparent Km for brassinin 248 ± 23 nM and apparent Vmax 478 ± 15 pmol mg^-1 min^-1; minor side-product overlap with CYP71CR1 (~6% spirobrassinol of total products); first characterized member of new CYP71CR subfamily
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bra009149 was identified from elicitor-induced RNA-seq as a strongly upregulated CYP candidate. 2. Recombinant enzyme converted brassinin to cyclobrassinin in an NADPH-dependent reaction. 3. Isotope-labeling supported an epoxidation/S-heterocyclization mechanism. 4. Gene expression correlated with induced cyclobrassinin accumulation in leaves.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Bra005870; GenBank TPA BK009360–BK009375 (CYP71CR family sequences)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Rapid Cycling Brassica rapa (allele chosen by comparison with Chiifu reference genome)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Rapid Cycling Brassica rapa; comparison cultivar Chiifu-401-42
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative S-heterocyclization of brassinin to spirobrassinol, likely via a common epoxy-brassinin intermediate followed by sulfur attack at C3
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Spirobrassinin branch of cabbage phytoalexin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream tailoring step from brassinin toward spirobrassinin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key dedicated pathway step established; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense against pathogens; dietary crucifer metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of spirobrassinol, the likely immediate precursor of spirobrassinin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate identification; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae WAT11 with ATR1; in vitro enzyme assay with NADPH; LC-MS/MS product analysis; kinetic analysis; qRT-PCR/metabolite time-course correlation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in Brassica rapa leaves after elicitation, with late and Psm-specific induction pattern
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript abundance correlates with spirobrassinin accumulation; both induced late and specifically by Psm
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ATR1 (heterologous CYP reductase partner in yeast expression)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen elicitation with Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola (Psm); weaker/no specific induction by flg22 for the spirobrassinin branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for engineering spirobrassinin branch flux in crucifers, but likely requires an additional dehydrogenation step to complete spirobrassinin formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only trace spirobrassinin formed in vitro, indicating another enzyme likely catalyzes dehydrogenation of spirobrassinol; apparent Km for brassinin < 1 µM; minor product overlap with CYP71CR2 (~4% cyclobrassinin of total products)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bra005870 was identified as an elicitor-induced CYP paralog lacking clear Arabidopsis homolog. 2. Recombinant enzyme converted brassinin into a product consistent with spirobrassinol in an NADPH-dependent manner. 3. Spirobrassinol is inferred to be the immediate precursor of spirobrassinin, implying an additional downstream enzyme. 4. Gene expression and spirobrassinin accumulation were both late and Psm-specific.
